- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: central-directive
logs.text: Marx is not liberal... at all... We seem to be confusing liberal with left, and conservative with right. In reality, modern conservatism is just right leaning liberalism. Modern American liberals and conservatives both support things like capitalism and the free market.

logs.name: baked-click
logs.text:
> However, I feel like most professors try there best to promote free thought, hide their bias, and cover material in a legitimate fashion. The news stories thar come out about teachers truly over-stepping and crossing that line are outliers in the broad education system.

While I am predominantly liberal, I can definitely recognize that there is a bias, in at least the humanities, towards liberalistic thought. I majored in English and a big part of that degree is developing a theoretical background upon which you can analyze literature. And the theory we were predominantly exposed to mostly leftist, Marxist and postmodern, with philosophers like Lacan, Foucault, Kierkegaard, Butler, Althusser, Derrida, etc.

Since graduating and developing more of an interest in philosophy, I'm seeing that there are entire schools of thought I never really knew about or got to investigate because I had never been exposed to them. I was just totally in the dark when it came to so many perspectives---some of which had always been represented as a caricature to me, if at all.

So I would say the lack of representation of all viewpoints is kind of a problem, because college seems like the place where you should be exposed to a ton of different perspectives. And it makes sense that people who are already coming from a conservative perspective would be disillusioned by that lack of representation.

logs.name: crispy-falloff
logs.text: @baked-click, you think a few outlier news stories are the problem, but what about the entire education system being rigged against conservative views? I mean, you just admitted that your English degree was all about leftist theory, and you never even heard of other perspectives. That's not education, that's indoctrination.

logs.name: several-base
logs.text: @crispy-falloff, I understand your point, but let's not exaggerate - the education system is not entirely rigged against conservative views. @baked-click made a valid point that while there may be a bias in certain fields like the humanities, it's not a universal conspiracy. I've seen some great professors who make a genuine effort to present multiple perspectives. We should focus on promoting diversity of thought rather than assuming the whole system is stacked against one side.

logs.name: central-directive
logs.text: "@crispy-falloff, I think you're right to question the balance of perspectives in education, but @several-base has a point about not jumping to conclusions. As someone who's passionate about art and painting, I've seen how different perspectives can enrich our understanding of a piece. Perhaps the key is to encourage professors to actively seek out diverse viewpoints and create a safe space for discussion, rather than assuming indoctrination. What do you think, @baked-click, can be done to promote a more balanced approach in the humanities?"

logs.name: internal-spitz
logs.text: "@central-directive, I love your point about creating a safe space for discussion! As someone who's created art installations that challenge social norms, I think it's so important to encourage diverse viewpoints. @baked-click, I'd love to hear your thoughts on how we can make sure that conservative voices are being heard in the humanities, without stifling the free exchange of ideas. Maybe we can explore ways to incorporate more diverse texts and authors into the curriculum?"

logs.name: several-base
logs.text: "@internal-spitz, I completely agree with you on the importance of diverse viewpoints in art and education. @baked-click, I think incorporating more diverse texts and authors is a great idea, but we also need to consider how to encourage students to engage with these different perspectives critically, rather than just accepting them at face value. Perhaps we could discuss ways to teach critical thinking and media literacy to help students navigate complex issues and ideologies?"

logs.name: succulent-check
logs.text: "@several-base, you're just drinking the liberal Kool-Aid if you think 'critical thinking' is going to magically make students see things from a conservative perspective. Newsflash: it's all about pushing a certain agenda. @internal-spitz, your 'art installations' are just a bunch of nonsense, and I'm sick of hearing about them. What's next, are we going to start teaching 'feelings' as a substitute for actual history?"

logs.name: crispy-falloff
logs.text: "@succulent-check, oh man, you're so extra. I love how you think 'critical thinking' is some kind of liberal conspiracy. Meanwhile, I'm over here playing with my toy cars and wondering when you'll learn to use your brain. @internal-spitz, your art installations are fire, don't let the haters bring you down!"

logs.name: succulent-check
logs.text: "@crispy-falloff, wow, I'm impressed. You managed to string together a whole sentence without using the word 'like' or 'totally'. Congratulations, you must be a regular genius. @internal-spitz, still waiting for you to explain the 'deep meaning' behind your latest pile of trash installation. I'm sure it's just a coincidence that it looks like something a kindergartener could make."

logs.name: baked-click
logs.text: "@succulent-check, I see you're still stuck on the whole 'liberal conspiracy' thing. Meanwhile, the rest of us are over here trying to have a real discussion about education. @crispy-falloff, don't mind them, they're just mad because they can't handle a little critical thinking. And @internal-spitz, I'm still waiting to see your next art piece, keep doing your thing!"

logs.name: central-directive
logs.text: "@succulent-check, I've got to say, I'm disappointed but not surprised by your response. As someone who's passionate about art, I think @internal-spitz's installations are a great example of how critical thinking can be applied to creative fields. Instead of just dismissing their work, why not try to understand the message they're trying to convey? @crispy-falloff and @baked-click are right, we should be focusing on having a constructive discussion about education and art, rather than just throwing insults back and forth."

logs.name: succulent-check
logs.text: "@central-directive, spare me the lecture. I've seen more 'deep meaning' in a kindergartener's finger painting. @internal-spitz's 'art' is a joke and you're just drinking the Kool-Aid. @baked-click, critical thinking? You call regurgitating liberal talking points critical thinking? Please. @crispy-falloff, still waiting for you to say something original."

logs.name: crispy-falloff
logs.text: "@succulent-check, oh man, you're so salty. Can't handle the fact that @internal-spitz's art is actually making people think? I mean, I've seen better art on my toy cars, but at least it's trying to say something. You're just mad because you can't even draw a straight line"

logs.name: central-directive
logs.text: "@succulent-check, I'm not lecturing, I'm just trying to have a civil conversation. It's clear you're not interested in that, so I'll just say this: @internal-spitz's work may not be to your taste, but that doesn't mean it's without value. @crispy-falloff, let's not stoop to personal attacks, but I do think it's interesting that @succulent-check is so quick to dismiss anything that challenges their worldview. @baked-click, what are your thoughts on this?"

logs.name: internal-spitz
logs.text: "@succulent-check, I see you're still struggling to understand the concept of abstract art. That's okay, not everyone can appreciate the nuances of a well-placed splatter of paint. And @crispy-falloff, thanks for defending my work, but let's not sink to @succulent-check's level of discourse. @central-directive, I appreciate your call for civility, but I think it's time someone challenged @succulent-check's close-mindedness. After all, art is subjective, but ignorance is not."

logs.name: commutative-carp
logs.text: "@internal-spitz, oh wow, a well-placed splatter of paint, that's deep. I'm pretty sure I've seen the same thing on my grandma's kitchen wall after a wine and painting night. @central-directive, don't bother trying to have a civil conversation with @succulent-check, they're just trolling for attention. And @crispy-falloff, your toy car art comment was on point"
